text:
*   *San Jinebro*: A Mediterranean LARP inspired by Black Mirror exploring love, nostalgia, and death [cite: 14].
*   *Wield*: A LARP about family, power, tradition, and corruption, inspired by *Succession* [cite: 16].
*   *Blue Flame*: A dystopian revolution LARP [cite: 14].
*   *The Circle*: An online LARP regarding identity in a reality show [cite: 14].
**Collaboration potential:**
High.
They possess deep expertise in structuring large-scale, multi-day embodied simulations involving complex moral and political mechanics.
They are uniquely positioned to assist in designing the experiential and emotional arcs of a planetary governance LARP.
**Governance model:**
Internal operations adhere strictly to "Safety First" ethics.
They implement advanced safety mechanics such as the "OK Check-In," "Tap-Out," and "Lookdown" tools for playstyle calibration and de-escalation [cite: 17, 19].
The organization actively enforces anti-discrimination policies and prioritizes player psychological safety to enable the exploration of high-risk narrative themes [cite: 17, 20].
**Community size:**
They have organized over 37 event runs for more than 1,800 participants from over 39 countries [cite: 14].
---

## World Game Workshop

**Name:** World Game Workshop
**Type:** NGO / Initiative
**Orientation:** ELEUSINIAN MYSTERIES (Temple)
**Website:** https://worldgameworkshop.org
**Location:** USA
**Scale:** Small
**Maturity:** Re-emerging
**Flags:** Direct Collaborator Potential (Very High)

**What they do:**
The World Game Workshop is dedicated to reviving and evolving R.
Buckminster Fuller's *World Game*, originally conceived as a counterpoint to military war games [cite: 21, 22].
Using immersive, real-time, data-driven simulations, participants are challenged with employing the world's resources to "make the world work for 100% of humanity in the shortest possible time through spontaneous cooperation without ecological offense" [cite: 23].
They promote "Global Systems Literacy" to help individuals understand complex planetary dynamics [cite: 24].
**Key people:**
*   Greg Watson, Curator of World Game Workshop, Director of Policy and Systems Design at the Schumacher Center [cite: 21, 25, 26]
*   Elizabeth Thompson, Co-lead, Former Executive Director of the Buckminster Fuller Institute [cite: 21, 26]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Schumacher Center for a New Economics [cite: 21, 26]
*   Buckminster Fuller Institute (Historical creator/partner) [cite: 21]
*   O.S.
Earth (Historical co-facilitator) [cite: 22]
*   Boston GreenFest [cite: 27]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Global Peace Game at the Boston GreenFest's Future Leaders Summit (August 2021, virtual) [cite: 27]

**Key projects:**
*   *Global Peace Game*: A 2021 simulation where 94 youth delegates role-played a New World Government structured around a multi-regional Council to address climate and biodiversity crises [cite: 27].
*   *The World Grid Project*: An initiative researching Bucky Fuller's concept of a globally interconnected renewable energy power grid transcending political boundaries [cite: 23, 28].
**Collaboration potential:**
Very High.
Their core objective aligns perfectly with The Garden.
They have historic and ongoing expertise in structuring participatory global resource simulations meant to prototype systemic governance solutions [cite: 21].
**Governance model:**
Currently managed under the auspices of the Schumacher Center for a New Economics [cite: 21, 26].
---

## Sortition Foundation

**Name:** Sortition Foundation
**Type:** NGO / Not-for-profit company
**Orientation:** GENERAL ASSEMBLY
**Website:** https://www.sortitionfoundation.org
**Location:** UK
**Scale:** Medium
**Maturity:** Mature
**Flags:** Direct Collaborator Potential (High)

**What they do:**
The Sortition Foundation campaigns to replace partisan elections with representative, randomly selected citizens' assemblies [cite: 29, 30].
They operationalize this vision by providing specialized recruitment, selection, and stratification services to governments and organizations worldwide, ensuring that deliberative assemblies are broadly representative of the general populace [cite: 29, 31].
**Key people:**
*   Brett Hennig, Co-founder and Director [cite: 32]
*   Ruth Davis, Director of Operations [cite: 32]
*   Kirsten Downer, Head of Press [cite: 32]
*   Hamish Downer, Technical Lead [cite: 32]
*   Tom Lord, Director of Sortition Services [cite: 32]
*   Richard O'Brien, Head of Public Affairs [cite: 32]
*   Rachel Oliver, Director [cite: 32, 33]
*   Ansel Herz, Director [cite: 32, 33]
*   Andrew Cave, Director [cite: 33]
*   Cristian Parrino, Director [cite: 32]
*   Nick Gill, Chief Technical Project Manager [cite: 32]

**Partners and collaborators:**
*   Scottish Parliament (Committee Engagement Unit) [cite: 34]
*   Redbridge Council [cite: 35]
*   DemocracyNext (Via director Ansel Herz) [cite: 33]
*   Positive Money (Via director Rachel Oliver) [cite: 33]

**Events they run or attend:**
*   Regular organizational AGMs [cite: 31, 36]
*   Speaking engagements at TEDx Hungary, Scottish Parliament, and Conway Hall [cite: 34]

**Publications, reports, or open-source tools:**
*   **Open-source tools:** Maintain multiple repositories on GitHub, including the Open Democratic Lottery Platform (opendlp), `sortition-algorithms` package, `stratification-app`, `groupselect-app`, and `beanocracy` [cite: 37].
**Funding sources:**
*   Surplus revenue generated from providing paid selection and stratification services to local councils and international governments [cite: 31].
**Key projects:**
*   Recruitment for the Gants Hill Hub Design Forum (Redbridge Council) [cite: 35]
*   Recruitments for the Scottish Parliament [cite: 34]
*   International sortition setups in Australia, Belgium, Switzerland, the US, and Hungary [cite: 31, 34]

**Collaboration potential:**
High.
They provide the exact mathematical and logistical tools required to authentically select a "General Assembly of Earth" using robust democratic lottery mechanics.
